hero.title: History, ethos, leadership, and safeguarding gathered with dignity
hero.lede: The parish's public materials present St Nick's as a place of worship, charity, synod listening, and careful safeguarding rooted in Augustinian hospitality.

intro.title: St Nicholas of Tolentino as the spiritual thread
intro.body: The parish's own mission language highlights prayer, humility, bread for the poor, and compassionate Augustinian witness. Those themes run through its worship, foodbank, refugee accompaniment, and pastoral welcome.
intro.imageAlt: Painting of St Nicholas of Tolentino.
intro.caption: The parish patron remains the key spiritual reference for worship and service.

cards.title: Mission and Ethos
paragraphs: The parish mission joins Eucharistic worship with welcome, justice, and accompaniment of refugees, asylum seekers, homeless neighbours, and people under financial pressure.
Its published outreach includes Borderlands, the Wild Goose Cafe, One25, Alabare CCC / The Well, and practical support through St Nicholas Bread.

cards.title: St Nicholas of Tolentino
paragraphs: The parish's visual and spiritual language draws on themes linked with St Nicholas of Tolentino: the star of hope, the lily of purity, bread for the poor, humility, and prayer.

cards.title: Parish History
paragraphs: The public gallery records baptisms, first Eucharist, confirmation, parish celebrations, and a 170 Anniversary archive that anchors the parish's recent historical memory.

cards.title: Parish Leadership
paragraphs: Parish priest details and day-to-day office contact information are kept in the shared parish settings so they stay consistent across the site.

cards.title: Synod Information
paragraphs: Synod reports and parish listening documents can be updated here whenever new files are published.

cards.title: Safeguarding
paragraphs: Safeguarding has its own page and is also linked from the footer for quick access.
